perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of England. The Parties hereby consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and consent that all personal service of process be made by certified mail or registered mail directed to the address stated herein and service so made shall be deemed to be completed upon actual receipt thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be construed in accordance with the laws of California, United States. The Parties hereby consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and consent that all personal service of process be made by certified mail or registered mail directed to the address stated herein and service so made shall be deemed to be completed upon mailing.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law to California while retaining the exclusive jurisdiction of English courts creates a conflict, as California courts would typically have jurisdiction over matters governed by California law, especially when it involves parties present in California. Furthermore, completing service upon mailing contradicts California law.
perturbation.law_explanation: California law requires acknowledgement of receipt for service by mail to be considered complete, or alternative methods like personal service if acknowledgement isn't received. The modified text stating service is complete upon mailing directly contradicts this requirement.

OF CIVIL ACTIONS [307 - 1062.34] ( Part 2 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 5. JURISDICTION AND SERVICE OF PROCESS [410.10 - 418.11] ( Title 5 added by Stats. 1969, Ch. 1610. ) CHAPTER 4. Service of Summons [413.10 - 417.40] ( Chapter 4 added by Stats. 1969, Ch. 1610. ) ARTICLE 3. Manner of Service of Summons [415.10 - 415.95] ( Article 3 added by Stats. 1969, Ch. 1610. ) 415.20. (a) In lieu of personal delivery of a copy of the summons and complaint to the person to be served as specified in Section 416.10, 416.20, 416.30, 416.40, or 416.50, a summons may be served by leaving a copy of the summons and complaint during usual office hours in his or her office or, if no physical address is known, at his or her usual mailing address, other than a United States Postal Service post office box, with the person who is apparently in charge thereof, and by thereafter mailing a copy of the summons and complaint by first-class mail, postage prepaid to the person to be served at the place where a copy of the summons and complaint were left. When service is effected by leaving a copy of the summons and complaint at a mailing address, it shall be left with a person at least 18 years of age, who shall be informed of the contents thereof. Service of a summons in this
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a legal contradiction by changing the governing law to California while retaining the exclusive jurisdiction of English courts, and by altering the service of process rules. California law requires acknowledgment of receipt for service by mail to be complete, and the changed text stipulates that service is complete upon mailing. This is a contradiction. CCP 415.20 outlines specific requirements for service by mail, implying that mere mailing is insufficient without proper delivery and acknowledgment, which the changed text contradicts.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties waive the right to trial by jury in any action, suit, proceeding or counterclaim of any kind arising out of or related to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree to waive the right to trial by jury in any action, suit, proceeding or counterclaim of any kind arising out of or related to this Agreement. This waiver applies to claims brought by either party, but does not apply to claims for unpaid wages.
perturbation.explanation: California law prohibits pre-dispute jury waivers in wage claim cases.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Constitution Article I, Section 16 guarantees the right to a jury trial. While parties can waive this right, it is against public policy to enforce pre-dispute jury waivers in wage claims, as these are considered unwaivable rights designed to protect employees.

perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a legal contradiction by including a waiver of jury trial for claims of unpaid wages. According to the Perturbation Explanation, California law prohibits pre-dispute jury waivers in wage claim cases. The Law Explanation states that it is against public policy to enforce pre-dispute jury waivers in wage claims, as these are considered unwaivable rights designed to protect employees. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 provides a search tool for California Codes, it does not contain the specific law cited in the explanation; however, the explanations themselves strongly suggest that the added clause contradicts established law and public policy.
